focusAreas: Verify sprintf is used properly (not string concatenation) for author/date
Verify all topics render as pills (not just the first)
Verify NO duplicate variable declarations exist in PHP
Verify TSX and PHP are still in sync after changes

tsx.dateLine: Line 250-254: sprintf() with translatable string 'Published on: %s'
tsx.notes: Both author and date use sprintf() correctly with translator comments

php.dateLine: Line 123-127: sprintf() with translatable string 'Published on: %s'
php.notes: Both author and date use sprintf() correctly with translator comments

sprintfUsage.notes: TSX and PHP use identical sprintf() patterns. No string concatenation found.

tsx.structure: Line 188-214: Wrapper div with 'flex flex-wrap gap-2', maps over allCategories array
tsx.loop: allCategories.map( ( cat ) => ... ) renders one pill per category
tsx.notes: Correctly loops through ALL categories, not just first

php.structure: Line 71-86: Wrapper div with 'flex flex-wrap gap-2', foreach loop over $categories
php.loop: foreach ( $categories as $category ) renders one pill per category
php.notes: Correctly loops through ALL categories, not just first

multipleTopicPills.testPageObservation: Test post has only 1 category ('Student Life'), so only 1 pill renders. This is correct behavior. Code is structured to render multiple pills when multiple categories exist.
multipleTopicPills.notes: Both TSX and PHP correctly loop through all categories. Wrapper div with flex-wrap gap-2 allows pills to wrap on multiple lines if needed.

noDuplicateVariables.notes: PHP no longer manually extracts attributes. Uses auto-extracted variables directly with null coalescing (??) for safe defaults.

structureMatches: Section element with conditional firstAccentColor class
Hero inner div with background image layer
Content layer with same padding calculations
Lower group: mt-12 flex flex-col gap-1
Author and date lines with sprintf()
tsxPhpSync.notes: TSX and PHP structure remains perfectly synced after revision. All changes applied consistently to both files.

build.notes: Build completes successfully with expected asset size warnings only.

lint.notes: Only minor React Hook dependency warnings in PostHero.tsx (line 88). These are acceptable - the dependency array uses allCategories.map().join() which is a common pattern for array dependencies. All other lint warnings are in unrelated blocks.

registration.notes: All required files exist. Block registration unchanged from previous QA.

attributes.notes: Attributes unchanged. showExcerpt and useTopicColor both default to false.

edgeCases.notes: Empty state handling correct: pills wrapper only renders when categories exist, excerpt only renders when showExcerpt=true AND excerpt exists, background image only renders when featured image exists.

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements - block displays static post content.

patternCompliance.notes: Uses semantic section element. Uses h1 for page heading. Background image has role='presentation' and alt=''.

semanticHTML.notes: Proper h1 heading, semantic section, paragraph tags for text. Div wrapper for pills with flex layout.

keyboardNavigation.notes: No interactive elements requiring keyboard navigation.

accessibility.notes: Accessibility unchanged from previous QA. All patterns correct.

markupDuplication.notes: No responsive duplication. Uses responsive utilities without duplicating markup.

tsxPhpSync.notes: TSX and PHP are perfectly synced after revision. All changes applied consistently: sprintf() usage, multi-category loop, wrapper div structure, and no duplicate attribute extraction.

innerBlocks.reason: Block does not support inner blocks.

specCompliance.notes: Test page has 4 block variations. All render correctly per REST API content field.

previousIssuesResolved.original: PHP manually extracted attributes from $attributes array
previousIssuesResolved.resolution: RESOLVED - Lines 10-11 removed. PHP now uses auto-extracted variables directly.
previousIssuesResolved.verified: Grep search confirms no $showExcerpt = $attributes or $useTopicColor = $attributes found in PHP.

previousIssuesResolved.original: PHP missing @var docblock for camelCase variables
previousIssuesResolved.resolution: RESOLVED - Docblock added at lines 5-12 with @var annotations for $anchor, $showExcerpt, $useTopicColor, $content, and $block.
previousIssuesResolved.verified: Grep search confirms '@var bool $showExcerpt' present in PHP line 7.

previousIssuesResolved.original: TSX had unused variables: postId and featuredMediaId
previousIssuesResolved.resolution: RESOLVED - Variables removed. TSX now only extracts 'post' from useSelect (line 35), not postId. featuredMediaUrl is used directly.
previousIssuesResolved.verified: No unused variable lint errors for PostHero.tsx.

revisionImprovements.impact: Author and date strings now use proper sprintf() formatting with translator comments, making them easier to translate to other languages.
revisionImprovements.implementation: Both TSX (lines 241-245, 250-254) and PHP (lines 110-114, 123-127) use sprintf(__('By: %s', 'takt'), $author) pattern.

revisionImprovements.impact: Posts with multiple categories will now display all category pills, not just the first one. Pills wrap to multiple lines if needed.
revisionImprovements.implementation: PHP uses foreach loop (lines 72-85), TSX uses .map() (lines 190-208). Both wrapped in flex flex-wrap gap-2 container (PHP line 71, TSX line 188).

revisionImprovements.impact: PHP code is cleaner and follows theme conventions. Reduces confusion about where variables come from.
revisionImprovements.implementation: Removed $showExcerpt = $attributes['showExcerpt'] and $useTopicColor = $attributes['useTopicColor']. Uses auto-extracted variables with ?? null coalescing.

passedChecks: sprintf() used correctly in both TSX and PHP for author/date
Topic pills loop through ALL categories in both TSX and PHP
Pills wrapper uses flex flex-wrap gap-2 for multi-line support
No duplicate $showExcerpt or $useTopicColor variable declarations in PHP
PHP has proper @var docblock for camelCase attributes
TSX and PHP structure perfectly synced
Conditional rendering matches between TSX and PHP
Background image uses role='presentation' and alt=''
Semantic HTML with section, h1, paragraph tags
